title: Discovery Service: Credential Distribution and Rotation (Pull Model) for Sentinels
description: Implement the endpoints and logic for sentinels to download their active credentials, retrieve incremental updates since a timestamp, and manage rotation with a grace period (both old and new credential accepted simultaneously). Distribution is scoped strictly per sentinel — only credentials belonging to the authenticated sentinel are returned. Rotated credentials pass through a deprecated_until window before the old credential becomes invalid.
objectives: Secure, scoped credential delivery — a sentinel only receives its own credentials
Incremental sync (since parameter + ETag/If-Modified-Since) to minimize bandwidth for polling sentinels
Rotation grace period: overlapping credential validity allows zero-downtime key/credential turnover

steps: Step 1 - Sentinel Credential Feed Endpoint: GET /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/credentials. Auth: sentinel bearer token (issued at onboarding) OR security-admin. Query params: since=<ISO-8601 timestamp> (optional, for incremental sync), type=<credential_type> (optional filter), status=active|deprecated (default: active,deprecated combined). Returns list of VC metadata + JWT blobs for credentials that belong to this sentinel's DID + env + service_id.

Step 2 - Scoping Filter (Security-Critical): The SQL query MUST filter by subject_did = sentinel_record.did AND env = sentinel_record.env AND service_id = sentinel_record.service_id. Never return credentials from a different env or service to a calling sentinel. Verify that the requesting sentinel's bearer token subject matches the sentinel_id in the URL. Mismatches → 403 FORBIDDEN.

Step 3 - Incremental Sync: If since parameter is provided, return only credentials where issued_at > since OR (status='deprecated' AND deprecated_until > since). This allows sentinels to poll every N minutes and receive only changed/new records. If no since is provided, return full active set.

Step 4 - ETag / If-Modified-Since Caching: Compute a feed ETag as SHA-256(sorted jti list of returned credentials). Return ETag header. If client sends If-None-Match and it matches → return 304. Also support If-Modified-Since: last_credential_issued_at. Reduces load for polling sentinels with no changes.

Step 5 - Response Schema: { credentials: [ { credential_id, jti, credential_type, subject_did, expires_at, status, deprecated_until (nullable), jwt_vc } ], fetched_at, next_poll_after (ISO-8601, hint for sentinel polling interval) }. jwt_vc is the full signed JWT string. If encrypted_payload is stored → decrypt with SecretStorage and return plaintext JWT to authenticated sentinel.

Step 6 - Rotation Support: When a new VC is issued to replace an existing one (via reissue_on_rotation in TASK-028): (a) old credential status stays 'active' → changes to 'deprecated', (b) deprecated_until = now() + ROTATION_GRACE_PERIOD (default 300 seconds, configurable). During grace period, feed returns both old (deprecated) and new (active) credentials. After deprecated_until, sweeper changes old status to 'expired'. Sentinels must accept both during grace period.

Step 7 - Rotation Grace Period Sweeper: Background task (runs every 60 seconds). SELECT * FROM credentials WHERE status='deprecated' AND deprecated_until < now(). Batch update status='expired'. Log count. No audit event per credential (bulk logged at system level).

Step 8 - Admin Trigger Rotation Endpoint: POST /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/credentials/{credential_id}/rotate. Auth: security-admin. Body: { new_expires_in_days (optional, defaults to original TTL) }. Calls CredentialIssuerService.reissue_on_rotation(credential_id). Sets deprecated_until on old record. Returns new credential metadata. Writes audit event: action='credential_rotated', actor, old_jti, new_jti.

Step 9 - Forced Full Sync: GET /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/credentials/sync-full ignores ETag and since, always returns the full active+deprecated set. Used after sentinel recovery from outage or credential store corruption. Requires sentinel bearer token. Rate limited: max 10 full syncs per sentinel per hour.

Step 10 - Delivery Receipts (Optional): If CREDENTIAL_DELIVERY_RECEIPTS=true in settings: after sentinel fetches credentials, a background task records delivery: update credentials table feedback column delivered_at=now() per jti. Used for operational visibility (admin can see if a sentinel has fetched its latest credentials). Does not affect authorization logic.

Step 11 - Sentinel Authentication for Credential Fetch: The sentinel must authenticate using the bearer token embedded in the onboarding bundle (a short-lived JWT issued by Discovery at onboarding + a DID-signed request header). Token TTL: 24h, renewable via POST /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/auth/renew (DID PoP required). This ensures that even if a sentinel token leaks, it expires within 24h.

files.services/discovery/src/discovery/service: Feed query, scoping, ETag, rotation logic
files.services/discovery/src/discovery/tasks/r: Background task for deprecated → expired transition
files.services/discovery/src/discovery/schemas: Pydantic response schemas for credential feed

api_endpoints.response_304: Not Modified (no changes since last ETag)

error_handling.condition: Requesting sentinel ID does not match token subject
error_handling.message: You are not authorized to access credentials for sentinel with this ID

error_handling.condition: credential_id does not belong to sentinel_id
error_handling.message: Credential not found or does not belong to this sentinel
Maximum full syncs (10/hour) exceeded. Use incremental sync with 'since' parameter.
Credential is already being rotated. Wait for deprecated_until to expire.

security_constraints: Strict subject_did + env + service_id scoping on all DB queries — cross-sentinel credential access impossible at query level
Sentinel bearer token validated on every request — expired tokens rejected without fallback
JWT-VC payload returned to sentinel delivered only over TLS — no plaintext on wire
Audit event written for every credential fetch: actor=sentinel_did, action='credentials_fetched', count=N
deprecated_until grace period: configurable per environment — shorter in prod (default 300s), longer in dev (default 1800s)
Encrypted credentials in DB: decrypted only in-process, never logged

acceptance_criteria: GET /credentials with a sentinel token for sentinel A never returns credentials for sentinel B
Incremental sync with since parameter returns only credentials issued after that time
Second GET with same ETag returns 304
Rotation creates new credential with new jti, marks old as deprecated with deprecated_until
Rotation sweeper transitions deprecated credential to expired after deprecated_until passes (integration test)
Full sync endpoint respects 10/hour rate limit per sentinel
